preview_text:
The model favours Algeria to win at 45%, with Jordan trailing at 25%. The underdog has a quarter chance of victory, while a draw — the second-likeliest outcome — sits at 30%, suggesting competitive depth in the matchup.

The three-way spread indicates a moderately open contest. Algeria holds a meaningful edge over Jordan, but the draw probability of 30% reflects meaningful uncertainty in the result. No single outcome dominates overwhelmingly; the gap between the favourite and the draw is only 15 percentage points, indicating the match carries genuine competitive tension rather than a heavily sloped profile.

The market diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks price Algeria at 63%, a gap of 18 percentage points above the model, and compress Jordan to 14% — 11 points below it. Polymarket prices align closely with the broader sportsbook view. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is more accurate, but the consistent lean toward Algeria across market venues suggests material daylight between algorithmic and consensus forecasts.
